column36: If I knew the Mastermind were the Illuminati, I wouldn't have chosen Bishop. An interesting setup to mess with! Both builds were able to KO cards with ease, allowing them to ramp with little to no issues. Got a Rare on one side, too! What a way to close the Tournament. Thanks for hosting!~

I am still trying to decide whether I wanted to give this a 4 or a 5, so it is probably good that we do not get options to pick 4 and a half. :) I went with 5, because in both games I never felt certain of the outcome. My first game only had one card left in the villain deck when I finally beat it. The change in heroes for the second attempt helped, and I think the second mastermind was better suited for my choices overall. The draw came up better for me in the second game as well. Like most of the ratings, my final decision comes down to whether I would play again. For this one, I absolutely will. Good job incorporating the new scheme!
1st attempts decks were better but the mm was harder.

I had a lot of fun play testing this scheme. First game was plagued with Twists and barely survived with 8 bodyguards sitting for too long of a stretch. Also since both of my decks heavily used card draw, the MM for attempt 1 was especially brutal. For game 2, I was a bit more lucky and with an Ascended Ego's help and was able to get through all 5 strikes before releasing the true MM. Red King was a bit easier without any strikes. In game 2 P1 went with a discard Angel/Darkhawk deck while P2 went for a draw and Smash deck. Unfortunately was never able to get Hulk is King in the deck for P2 which was the main reason I brought that hero, but it worked fine while P1 did most of the work. I will say Rival Overlords is one of my least favorite villain groups because they feel samey and it's rare for more than one to Ascend, but they were a lot more fun with this scheme and there was definitely a strategy to let one of them ascend to better direct the strikes than more bodyguards.

Well I had a lot of fun. Our first 5 turns were two Twists and three strikes. By the time Jester came out the first time all of the henchmen had been played and the Apoc was out of strikes. We pulled the other Jester put them on the bottom of the scry. So the game was pretty stress free after we didn't see a twist in the immediate future.

We rushed to seven KO'ed bystanders and then held the line until Jean did Jean-stuff and beat the heck out of the MM. Hopefully we hold the line too and hang out in the top 15 overall!

column36: This one had a lot of fun combos for hand 2. I mixed strong guy, colossus and human torch which really let me play with the wound-draw cards mechanic. This one was pretty hard.  All those Demonic Bargains bombarding with Wounds.  I tried to leverage the expected wounds with my hero choices, but I just didn't ever seem to get enough of the wound removal cards to keep them from really bogging me down.  Winning the second game was fortunate the seventh twist didn't hit. I've said many times that I don't really like the games where scheme X just ends the game.  That's how I lost my first game, scheme 7 hit earlier.

Thought this one wouldn't be too hard, but I was wrong lol. Really good synergy, I just didn't get they key cards in either attempt until too late in the game. In one attempt, I never saw Human Torch's wound KO card which really slowed things down. Not too unfun though, and a great tournament overall. Thanks!

I went for M and vishanti thinking I would lean into the wounds in the setup. Turns out if you never buy the cards that get rid of wounds, they clog up your deck pretty badly. First game was a loss despite an early Colossus rare due to some really unlucky demonic bargains discarding him instead of letting me draw him. Both games had several early twists making most of the city very difficult to fight at any stage of the game. I had to make many risky bargains and take many early wounds to keep it clear and even that wasn't enough.
We had unlucky draws, 4 twists in 11 turns was hard. Plus both times our thinning the deck cards didn't show. But we had fun and enjoyed it and the whole tournament. Thanks for all you do.

This setup seems very simple yet was very tough. Ended up losing both games but lasted longer and had better points per turn on game 2. The scheme twists really ramp up the difficulty when trying to fight very quickly even with being able to use a combo of recruit and attack. Enjoyed the tournament as always and will be waiting eagerly for the next one!

Brought banshee for piercing energy, and wonder man for rotating the HQ. 
Got lucky with many cape killers early on (after two master strikes in the first two round). Less recruit but ko was nice early game. Left hand went red, got wasp rare quite early. Right hand went blue and could get britain 7 cost via using wonderman 5 cost. City still got to 5 villains but after clearing that bottleneck it was easy and fast. Right hand also got into piercing energy (initially thought that the emperor is 7 vp, then realized its only 5 so Banshee got a two card combo, although often I needed that for villains.)  
Future ultron brought away wonderman rare unfortunately. So a fast total annihilation was delayed and got a bit sluggish mid game. Still finished turn 27. May be there will be some faster players. 
Also could not abuse invisible woman enough, only 3 extra bysranders last turn. Overall fun, fast game. 
Also almost equal vp for the two hands which did not happen since my very first game in the tournaments a couple of years ago.

Was also able to grab 3 ultimates early to end the game quicker with the Emperor's Circle.

I really liked the cost-and-HQ focused gameplay here! The anxiety of recruiting, not knowing if the next card would match the Scheme's cards, and also getting to play around with Piercing Energy (which I rarely have) result in a really thrilling time!

Absolutely brutal. I felt like I had a huge disadvantage this week playing with (what felt like) a really hard scheme with a brand new mechanic for me since I don't have that set. Maybe I just picked terrible heroes, but again, I just felt totally out of my depth.
Lost game 1 at the 27th round with the 7th scheme twist drawn. Lost game 2 at the 15th round with the 7th twist. 
I actually managed to build a decent deck the first game. Almost all grey cards gone and 8-12 attack per turn. I probably could have won in one or two more turns. I enjoyed it enough to try again and hope for better luck, but the second game was just ridiculous. Basically a twist every other card draw. No fun at all.
Maybe I could enjoy this if I better understood how to work around the scheme mechanic or with the perfect hero choice, but this one felt like a total bomb. Rough way to end a tourney.

column36: Morbius was great here with his blood frenzy cards able to generate big recruit and attack.

That's a wrap! Fun setup to end the tourney with. Thanks, Kylah, for tons of fun. I got to debut a lot of new-to-me content this tourney, and it was great to continue to do so in my last setup. I acquired Midnight Sons and Weapon X during the tourney, and it was nice to feature Midnight Sons in this one. Morbius' Blood Frenzy can really scale nicely later into the game. Hulk 2099 was interesting in the setup were sometimes it might be advantageous to take a wound from a master strike (to then send undercover), but I found his payoff to be really conditional. It depends on getting wounds, and sending them undercover, AND having payoff cards for doing so. It's interesting but requires too many pieces to fall into place to really go off - particularly with another hero added to the setup and the HQ getting deleted. Something that was a unique byproduct was that I found myself shattering Frenzy a couple times (despite not having enough attack to fight her, even reduced), so that I could try to give the Hulk deck some wounds. Stepford Cuckoos is also really interesting, but I just find it hard to get a late game payoff with them. They just always make mediocre numbers for me later in the game and depend on other heroes to get big numbers, but then it's easy for those other heroes to mess up her Formation payoffs. Interesting in the setup how the diminishing HQ made it tricky to manage Moonlight at times. The scheme can be kind of annoying though. It's an obvious tension with the scheme to lose out on recruiting options as the game progresses, but it's obnoxious when the reduced HQ gets clogged with expensive rares. Fun game, fun tourney, and can't wait for the next one!

I really dislike Exodus and had no fun this week.   The suggested hero choices for my first playthrough were useless.   Most of the game it was neither day nor night, making Morbius pretty bad and I never got to see the 2 cost Stepford making 4 copies of the 3 cost blue card dead weight.  My first game I had 6 Scheme Twists by turn 17 with 2 Master Strikes.
Fun way to end the tournament! Thank you for making this possible!

I think the first game, I was building for certain hero cards that just didn't come up. I want to try them again. I just wanted to see if I was missing something and try the recommended first. I still don't like having recommended heroes, makes me feel dumb. But, tbf, I am dumb lol. Second game, heroes felt too attack focused to me. Got good attack numbers but couldn't shatter enough. Will try again, fun game overall. Fun scheme and mastermind.

This one ended up being a bit of a slog for us. I hate to rate games low when we lost, but both of our games ended up with the same issue: closing the gap on hitting the MM just took us too long. The first game we very easily controlled the board, and we're KO'ing plenty of stuff, but even then we just couldn't swing on the guy consistently enough to close it out before we got a (fairly early) 8th twist. For our second game I was only one MM hit away from ending it before the final twist, but my wife just couldn't get a hit in on the MM again. Overall it just felt like a slog, and even though we were so close round two, we lost on both. A bummer for closing out the tournament, but we still had lots of fun. Thank you for putting all of this together, and for everyone who participated!
